content.text: Invoices

An Azure invoice is the monthly billing document that records all charges, credits, and adjustments for a billing period. The scope and structure of the invoice depend on the billing account type: Microsoft Customer Agreement (MCA) invoices are scoped to a billing profile and show invoice sections as line groups; Enterprise Agreement (EA) invoices cover an entire enrollment; MOSP (pay-as-you-go) invoices are per subscription.
Understanding how to retrieve, parse, and reconcile invoices programmatically is essential for finance automation, chargeback reporting, and auditability.

attrs.alt: Azure invoice lifecycle: period close, generation, delivery, payment

content.text: Invoice Anatomy and Delivery Timeline

Azure invoices are typically generated 3–5 business days after the billing period closes. For MCA billing profiles, the invoice PDF is emailed to all addresses configured on the billing profile and is also available in the Azure Portal under Cost Management + Billing.
Billing Account Type
Invoice Scope
Line Item Grouping
Delivery
MCA
Per billing profile
Invoice sections → subscriptions → resources
Email + portal PDF, CSV via API
EA
Per enrollment
Departments → accounts → subscriptions
EA Portal + API
MPA (CSP)
Per customer
Subscriptions
Partner Center + API
MOSP (PAYG)
Per subscription
Resource groups → resources
Email + portal PDF
The billing period for most MCA accounts is the calendar month (day 1–last day). Custom invoice periods are available for some agreements.
Listing and Downloading Invoices via Azure CLI

# List recent invoices for an MCA billing account
az billing invoice list \
  --account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --profile-name "<billing-profile-id>" \
  --output table

# Download the PDF for a specific invoice
az billing invoice download \
  --account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --profile-name "<billing-profile-id>" \
  --invoice-name "T000123456" \
  --download-urls pdf

# List invoices for a subscription (MOSP)
az billing invoice list \
  --subscription "<subscription-id>" \
  --output table
Retrieving Invoices via the Billing REST API

The Billing REST API exposes invoices as first-class resources. Each invoice has a `status` (Due, OverDue, Paid, Void), a `dueDate`, and a `totalAmount`. The API also provides pre-signed download URLs valid for 1 hour.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"

# List invoices for the past 12 months
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"invoices?api-version=2020-05-01"
    f"&periodStartDate=2024-01-01&periodEndDate=2024-12-31"
)
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
for invoice in resp.json().get("value", []):
    p = invoice["properties"]
    print(
        f"{invoice['name']}  "
        f"status={p.get('status'):<8}  "
        f"due={p.get('dueDate', 'N/A')[:10]}  "
        f"total={p.get('totalAmount', {}).get('value', 0):>10,.2f} "
        f"{p.get('totalAmount', {}).get('currency', '')}"
    )
Downloading the Invoice PDF Programmatically

import requests
import os
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def download_invoice_pdf(
    billing_account_name: str,
    billing_profile_name: str,
    invoice_name: str,
    output_dir: str = "."
) -> str:
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    # Request a download URL (long-running operation)
    url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
        f"invoices/{invoice_name}/download?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json={"downloadType": "Invoice"})

    if resp.status_code == 202:
        import time
        location = resp.headers.get("Location")
        for _ in range(12):
            poll = requests.get(location, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
            if poll.status_code == 200:
                download_url = poll.json().get("url")
                break
            time.sleep(5)
        else:
            raise TimeoutError("Download URL generation timed out")
    elif resp.status_code == 200:
        download_url = resp.json().get("url")
    else:
        resp.raise_for_status()

    # Fetch the PDF using the pre-signed URL (no auth header needed)
    pdf_resp = requests.get(download_url)
    pdf_resp.raise_for_status()

    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{invoice_name}.pdf")
    with open(output_path, "wb") as f:
        f.write(pdf_resp.content)
    print(f"Saved: {output_path} ({len(pdf_resp.content):,} bytes)")
    return output_path


download_invoice_pdf("<billing-account-id>", "<billing-profile-id>", "T000123456")
Retrieving Line-Item Usage Details
The invoice PDF shows totals. For reconciliation, finance teams need per-resource line items. Azure exposes these through the UsageDetails REST API and Cost Management query API.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"
invoice_name = "T000123456"

# Fetch usage details for a specific invoice
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"invoices/{invoice_name}/"
    f"providers/Microsoft.Consumption/usageDetails"
    f"?api-version=2023-05-01&$top=100"
)

total_pages = 0
total_rows = 0
while url:
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    data = resp.json()
    items = data.get("value", [])
    total_rows += len(items)
    total_pages += 1
    for item in items[:3]:  # print first 3 per page for illustration
        p = item.get("properties", {})
        print(
            f"{p.get('date','')}  "
            f"{p.get('meterCategory',''):<25}  "
            f"{p.get('resourceName','')[:30]:<30}  "
            f"{p.get('costInBillingCurrency', 0):>10.4f}"
        )
    url = data.get("nextLink")  # pagination

print(f"\nTotal: {total_rows} line items across {total_pages} pages")
Exporting Usage Details to CSV

import requests
import csv
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def export_invoice_usage_csv(
    billing_account_name: str,
    billing_profile_name: str,
    invoice_name: str,
    output_csv: str
):
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
        f"invoices/{invoice_name}/"
        f"providers/Microsoft.Consumption/usageDetails"
        f"?api-version=2023-05-01&$top=1000"
    )

    FIELDS = [
        "date", "subscriptionName", "resourceGroup", "resourceLocation",
        "meterCategory", "meterName", "quantity", "unitPrice",
        "costInBillingCurrency", "invoiceSectionName", "tags"
    ]

    rows_written = 0
    with open(output_csv, "w", newline="") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=FIELDS, extrasaction="ignore")
        writer.writeheader()
        while url:
            resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
            for item in resp.json().get("value", []):
                props = item.get("properties", {})
                # Flatten tags dict to string
                tags = props.get("tags") or {}
                row = {k: props.get(k, "") for k in FIELDS}
                row["tags"] = ";".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in tags.items())
                writer.writerow(row)
                rows_written += 1
            url = resp.json().get("nextLink")

    print(f"Exported {rows_written} rows to {output_csv}")

export_invoice_usage_csv(
    "<billing-account-id>",
    "<billing-profile-id>",
    "T000123456",
    "invoice_T000123456_usage.csv"
)
Invoice Status Monitoring and Alert Automation
Overdue invoices can trigger account suspension. Monitoring invoice status programmatically and alerting finance teams before the due date prevents service interruption.

import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def find_overdue_invoices(billing_account_name: str) -> list:
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    # List all billing profiles
    profiles_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/billingProfiles"
        f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    profiles_resp = requests.get(profiles_url, headers=headers)
    profiles = profiles_resp.json().get("value", [])

    overdue = []
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    for profile in profiles:
        profile_name = profile["name"]
        invoices_url = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile_name}/"
            f"invoices?api-version=2020-05-01"
        )
        inv_resp = requests.get(invoices_url, headers=headers)
        for invoice in inv_resp.json().get("value", []):
            props = invoice.get("properties", {})
            status = props.get("status", "")
            due_date_str = props.get("dueDate", "")
            if status in ("Due", "PastDue", "OverDue") and due_date_str:
                due_date = datetime.fromisoformat(due_date_str.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
                days_overdue = (now - due_date).days
                overdue.append({
                    "invoice": invoice["name"],
                    "billing_profile": profile["properties"]["displayName"],
                    "status": status,
                    "due_date": due_date_str[:10],
                    "days_overdue": days_overdue,
                    "amount": props.get("totalAmount", {}).get("value", 0),
                    "currency": props.get("totalAmount", {}).get("currency", ""),
                })

    return sorted(overdue, key=lambda x: x["days_overdue"], reverse=True)


overdue = find_overdue_invoices("<billing-account-id>")
for inv in overdue:
    print(
        f"OVERDUE {inv['days_overdue']:>3}d | {inv['invoice']} | "
        f"{inv['billing_profile']} | {inv['amount']:>10,.2f} {inv['currency']}"
    )
Enterprise Agreement Invoice Retrieval

EA customers access invoices through the EA Portal or the Billing REST API. EA invoices aggregate all department and account charges into a single enrollment-level document, with department and account breakdowns available as separate usage detail files.
Listing EA Invoices via REST API

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "1234567"  # EA enrollment number (numeric)

# List invoices at the EA billing account level
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"invoices?api-version=2020-05-01"
    f"&periodStartDate=2024-01-01&periodEndDate=2024-12-31"
)
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
for invoice in resp.json().get("value", []):
    props = invoice.get("properties", {})
    amount = props.get("totalAmount", {})
    print(
        f"{invoice['name']}  "
        f"period={props.get('billingProfileDisplayName', 'N/A')}  "
        f"status={props.get('status'):<8}  "
        f"total={amount.get('value', 0):>12,.2f} {amount.get('currency', '')}"
    )
Downloading EA Usage Detail File

import requests
import time
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def download_ea_usage_csv(
    billing_account_name: str,
    invoice_name: str,
    output_path: str = "ea_usage.csv"
):
    """Download the full EA usage CSV for a given invoice (enrollment-level)."""
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    # Request generation of the usage file (async)
    url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"invoices/{invoice_name}/"
        f"download?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json={"downloadType": "UsageAndCharges"})

    if resp.status_code == 202:
        location = resp.headers.get("Location")
        for _ in range(20):
            poll = requests.get(location, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
            if poll.status_code == 200:
                download_url = poll.json()["url"]
                break
            time.sleep(10)
        else:
            raise TimeoutError("Usage file generation timed out after 200s")
    else:
        download_url = resp.json().get("url")

    # Stream to disk
    with requests.get(download_url, stream=True) as r:
        r.raise_for_status()
        with open(output_path, "wb") as f:
            for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=1024 * 1024):
                f.write(chunk)

    import os
    size_mb = os.path.getsize(output_path) / 1_048_576
    print(f"Downloaded: {output_path} ({size_mb:.1f} MB)")


download_ea_usage_csv("1234567", "202401", "ea_usage_202401.csv")
Chargeback Automation: Per-Department Cost Breakdown
MCA invoice sections map to cost centers; EA departments map to cost centers. The following workflow extracts per-department totals from usage details and generates a chargeback report for finance.

import requests
import csv
from collections import defaultdict
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def chargeback_report(
    billing_account_name: str,
    billing_profile_name: str,
    invoice_name: str,
    output_csv: str
):
    """
    Summarize invoice charges by invoice section (department) and resource group.
    Outputs a CSV for finance chargeback.
    """
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    auth = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
        f"invoices/{invoice_name}/"
        f"providers/Microsoft.Consumption/usageDetails"
        f"?api-version=2023-05-01&$top=1000"
    )

    # dept → rg → cost
    dept_rg: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(float))
    currency = "USD"

    while url:
        resp = requests.get(url, headers=auth)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        for item in data.get("value", []):
            p = item.get("properties", {})
            dept = p.get("invoiceSectionName", "Unassigned")
            rg   = p.get("resourceGroup", "(none)")
            cost = float(p.get("costInBillingCurrency") or 0)
            currency = p.get("billingCurrency", currency)
            dept_rg[dept][rg] += cost
        url = data.get("nextLink")

    rows = []
    for dept, rg_costs in sorted(dept_rg.items()):
        dept_total = sum(rg_costs.values())
        for rg, cost in sorted(rg_costs.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True):
            rows.append({
                "invoice": invoice_name,
                "department": dept,
                "resource_group": rg,
                "cost": round(cost, 2),
                "currency": currency,
                "department_total": round(dept_total, 2),
            })

    with open(output_csv, "w", newline="") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=rows[0].keys() if rows else [])
        writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(rows)

    grand_total = sum(r["cost"] for r in rows)
    print(f"Chargeback report: {len(rows)} rows, grand total {grand_total:,.2f} {currency}")
    print(f"Written: {output_csv}")

    # Print department summary to stdout
    dept_totals: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
    for row in rows:
        dept_totals[row["department"]] += row["cost"]
    print(f"\n{'Department':<35} {'Total':>12}")
    print("-" * 50)
    for dept, total in sorted(dept_totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True):
        print(f"{dept:<35} {total:>12,.2f}")


chargeback_report(
    "<billing-account-id>",
    "<billing-profile-id>",
    "T000123456",
    "chargeback_T000123456.csv"
)
Invoice Reconciliation: Matching Line Items to Budget Periods

Finance teams often need to confirm that the invoice total matches Cost Management query results for the same period. Discrepancies can arise from credits applied, reservation purchases, or rounding. The following script performs the comparison.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def reconcile_invoice_vs_cost_mgmt(
    billing_account_name: str,
    billing_profile_name: str,
    invoice_name: str,
    period_start: str,  # YYYY-MM-DD
    period_end: str,    # YYYY-MM-DD
) -> dict:
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }

    # 1. Invoice total from the invoice resource
    invoice_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
        f"invoices/{invoice_name}?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    inv_resp = requests.get(invoice_url, headers=headers)
    inv_props = inv_resp.json().get("properties", {})
    invoice_total = float(inv_props.get("totalAmount", {}).get("value", 0))
    currency = inv_props.get("totalAmount", {}).get("currency", "USD")

    # 2. Cost Management total for the same period
    scope = (
        f"/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}"
    )
    query_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com{scope}/"
        f"providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/query?api-version=2023-11-01"
    )
    query_body = {
        "type": "ActualCost",
        "timeframe": "Custom",
        "timePeriod": {"from": period_start, "to": period_end},
        "dataSet": {
            "granularity": "None",
            "aggregation": {
                "totalCost": {"name": "Cost", "function": "Sum"}
            }
        }
    }
    cost_resp = requests.post(query_url, headers=headers, json=query_body)
    cost_rows = cost_resp.json().get("properties", {}).get("rows", [])
    cost_total = float(cost_rows[0][0]) if cost_rows else 0.0

    delta = invoice_total - cost_total
    return {
        "invoice_name": invoice_name,
        "invoice_total": invoice_total,
        "cost_mgmt_total": cost_total,
        "delta": delta,
        "currency": currency,
        "reconciled": abs(delta) < 1.0,  # within $1 tolerance
    }

result = reconcile_invoice_vs_cost_mgmt(
    "<billing-account-id>",
    "<billing-profile-id>",
    "T000123456",
    "2024-01-01",
    "2024-01-31"
)
status = "OK" if result["reconciled"] else "MISMATCH"
print(
    f"[{status}] Invoice={result['invoice_total']:,.2f} "
    f"CostMgmt={result['cost_mgmt_total']:,.2f} "
    f"Delta={result['delta']:+,.2f} {result['currency']}"
)

Discrepancies of ±$0.01–$1.00 between the invoice total and Cost Management are common due to rounding. Larger deltas usually indicate credits, reservation purchases billed separately, or Marketplace charges excluded from cost queries.
Common Invoice Issues and How to Resolve Them
Issue
Cause
Resolution
Invoice total higher than budgeted
Unexpected resource creation, overage on Marketplace, or expiry of credits
Drill into UsageDetails filtered by ServiceName; check for credits applied in the prior period
Invoice not generated after billing period closes
Billing profile in warning/suspended state; new account not yet invoiced
Check billing profile status in the portal; wait 5 business days before opening a support ticket
PDF download URL returns 403
Pre-signed URL expired (valid 1 hour) or caller lacks Billing Profile Reader role
Re-request the download URL; verify RBAC assignment
UsageDetails pagination returns empty last page
nextLink present but points to an empty result set (known API behavior)
Stop iteration when the value array is empty, not when nextLink is absent
EA invoice CSV missing subscriptions
New subscriptions created after the period start may appear in next month's invoice
Cross-reference with the subscription list API for the period
Summary

Azure invoices are generated monthly per billing profile (MCA), per enrollment (EA), or per subscription (MOSP), and are available as PDFs via the portal or as line-item CSV data via the consumption API. The billing REST API provides endpoints for listing invoices, generating pre-signed PDF download URLs, and paginating through usage details. Finance automation typically combines invoice status monitoring (alerting on overdue) with per-invoice CSV export for reconciliation and chargeback reporting.
Invoice PDFs are delivered by email and portal ~3–5 business days after the billing period closes
MCA: one invoice per billing profile; invoice sections appear as grouped line items
UsageDetails API paginates via nextLink — always follow pagination for full export
Pre-signed download URLs from the download API expire after 1 hour; do not cache them
Monitor invoice status for Due/OverDue to prevent account suspension before finance can act
